questions.questionText: The structure of X is :
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: If the foot of the perpendicular from point (4, 3, 8) on the line $${L_1}:{{x - a} \over l} = {{y - 2} \over 3} = {{z - b} \over 4}$$, l $$\ne$$ 0 is (3, 5, 7), then the shortest distance between the line L1 and line $${L_2}:{{x - 2} \over 3} = {{y - 4} \over 4} = {{z - 5} \over 5}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If y = y(x) is the solution of the differential equation

$${{dy} \over {dx}}$$ + (tan x) y = sin x, $$0 \le x \le {\pi \over 3}$$, with y(0) = 0, then $$y\left( {{\pi \over 4}} \right)$$ equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A mosquito is moving with a velocity $$\overrightarrow v  = 0.5{t^2}\widehat i + 3t\widehat j + 9\widehat k$$ m/s and accelerating in uniform conditions. What will be the direction of mosquito after 2 s?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A body of mass 2 kg moves under a force of $$\left( {2\widehat i + 3\widehat j + 5\widehat k} \right)$$N. It starts from rest and was at the origin initially. After 4s, its new coordinates are (8, b, 20). The value of b is _____________. (Round off to the Nearest Integer)
questions.subject: Physics
Mathematics

questions.questionText: A force $$\overrightarrow F $$ = $${4\widehat i + 3\widehat j + 4\widehat k}$$ is applied on an intersection point of x = 2 plane and x-axis. The magnitude of torque of this force about a point (2, 3, 4) is ___________. (Round off to the Nearest Integer)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Consider a rectangle ABCD having 5, 7, 6, 9 points in the interior of the line segments AB, CD, BC, DA respectively. Let $$\alpha$$ be the number of triangles having these points from different sides as vertices and $$\beta$$ be the number of quadrilaterals having these points from different sides as vertices. Then ($$\beta$$ $$-$$ $$\alpha$$) is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Arrange the following metal complex/compounds in the increasing order of spin only magnetic moment. Presume all the three, high spin system.

(Atomic numbers Ce = 58, Gd = 64 and Eu = 63)

(a) (NH4)2[Ce(NO3)6]

(b) Gd(NO3)3 and

(c) Eu(NO3)3
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
For real numbers $$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$, $$\gamma$$ and $$\delta $$, if
$$\int {{{({x^2} - 1) + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {{{{x^2} + 1} \over x}} \right)} \over {({x^4} + 3{x^2} + 1){{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {{{{x^2} + 1} \over x}} \right)}}dx} $$

$$ = \alpha {\log _e}\left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}\left( {{{{x^2} + 1} \over x}} \right)} \right) + \beta {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{{\gamma ({x^2} + 1)} \over x}} \right) + \delta {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{{{x^2} + 1} \over x}} \right) + C$$

where C is an arbitrary constant, then the value of 10($$\alpha$$ + $$\beta$$$$\gamma$$ + $$\delta$$) is equal to ______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Identify the reagent(s) 'A' and condition(s) for the reaction
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement I : A cyclist is moving on an unbanked road with a speed of 7 kmh$$-$$1 and takes a sharp circular turn along a path of radius of 2m without reducing the speed. The static friction coefficient is 0.2. The cyclist will not slip and pass the curve. (g = 9.8 m/s2)

Statement II : If the road is banked at an angle of 45$$^\circ$$, cyclist can cross the curve of 2m radius with the speed of 18.5 kmh$$-$$1 without slipping.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
options: (A) Statement I is incorrect and statement II is correct
(B) Both statement I and statement II are true
(C) Statement I is correct and statement II is incorrect
(D) Both statement I and statement II are false
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Let A denote the event that a 6-digit integer formed by 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 without repetitions, be divisible by 3. Then probability of event A is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Consider the integral
$$I = \int_0^{10} {{{[x]{e^{[x]}}} \over {{e^{x - 1}}}}dx} $$,
where [x] denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to x. Then the value of I is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A swimmer can swim with velocity of 12 km/h in still water. Water flowing in a river has velocity 6 km/h. The direction with respect to the direction of flow of river water he should swim in order to reach the point on the other bank just opposite to his starting point is ____________$$^\circ$$. (Round off to the Nearest Integer) (Find the angle in degrees)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The secondary structure of protein is stabilised by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Consider the statistics of two sets of observations as follows :

Size Mean Variance
Observation I 10 2 2
Observation II n 3 1


If the variance of the combined set of these two observations is $${{17} \over 9}$$, then the value of n is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $${1 \over {16}}$$, a and b be in G.P. and $${1 \over a}$$, $${1 \over b}$$, 6 be in A.P., where a, b > 0. Then 72(a + b) is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics
